interview.note: Strategic brief with 3 user types, accessibility requirements, scale range, and design philosophy

urls.note: Analyzed for density and clarity patterns — progressive disclosure, semantic color coding, monospace for technical content
Analyzed for navigation patterns, workspace hierarchy, AI agent layer on top of docs

research.note: Enterprise team collaboration UX patterns, accessibility standards, RTL considerations

figmaFile.note: Existing frames show dashboard, feed, sidebar, onboarding flow — confirms product is past initial design phase

designSystem.note: Untitled UI PRO: 92 components, 71 icons. Note: current tokens don't include RTL-specific tokens — may need extension.

identity.name: TeamPulse
identity.tagline: Team collaboration for distributed teams at scale
identity.stage: Series B — prioritizing consistent patterns over pixel-perfect polish (user-specified)
identity.designPhilosophy: Consistent patterns > pixel-perfect. Ship quality through systems, not artisanship.

primary.label: Individual contributor (IC)
primary.description: Daily active user in feed and wiki. Needs fast creation and fast discovery.
goals: Stay current without drowning in notifications

secondary.label: Team lead
secondary.description: Needs at-a-glance team health: who's active, who's blocked, what decisions were made
secondary.homeScreen: Status dashboard with metrics, needs-attention section, and decisions log
secondary.keyMetric: Time to 'is my team healthy?' answer (<10 seconds)

tertiary.label: Org admin
tertiary.description: Manages workspace infrastructure: SSO, billing, compliance. Different mental model entirely.
tertiary.homeScreen: Admin console with workspace management, security, billing

informationArchitecture.navigation: Left sidebar with icon + label, differentiated by user type

primarySections.name: Home
primarySections.purpose: User-type-specific landing (feed OR dashboard OR admin console)

teamSize.note: Must work for teams of 5 and 5000 (user-specified)

requirements: Color must not be the only status indicator
All metrics must have text labels
accessibility.note: WCAG AA explicitly required by user

internationalization.note: RTL support explicitly required — affects all layout decisions

frames.name: Deep Dive variations
frames.note: 4 variants showing different content density approaches

gaps: No team lead dashboard exists yet

designContext.currentFocus: Differentiated home screens per user type

keyTakeaways: Progressive disclosure through 9 typography levels — prevents cognitive overload
Semantic color coding for project status (red/green) but always paired with text labels
Monospace for technical content — reduces cognitive switching
Separation of concerns: projects, issues, teams as distinct layers
Performance-first: adaptive density for varied devices and contexts
Sidebar navigation with icon+label is the standard
AI agent layer on top of collaboration infrastructure
